name: Running circular from Onda to Les Penyes Aragoneses
name_original: Running circular desde Onda hasta Les Penyes Aragoneses
region_intro: Jogging routes around Onda offer a diverse terrain for runners, characterized by its hilly landscapes and historical landmarks. The region features a mix of trails that ascend to viewpoints, pass by ancient castles, and wind through natural areas. Runners can expect varied elevations, providing both challenging climbs and more moderate paths. The natural environment around Onda supports a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around Onda The most popular jogging route is Running circular from Onda to Les Penyes Aragoneses, a 11.5 miles (18.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through rocky hills. Another top favourite among local runners is Onda Castle – Sonella Bridge loop from Onda, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route offers views of Onda Castle and follows the Sonella Bridge, providing a mix of historical sights and natural scenery. Local runners also love the Santa Bàrbera – el Montí loop from Onda, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through the Santa Bàrbera and el Montí areas, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. This difficult route includes substantial climbs through the local hills. Jogging around Onda is defined by historical landmarks, varied hill climbs, and natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Onda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Onda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Onda?
A: There are over 70 running routes around Onda, offering a wide variety of terrain and experiences for joggers of all levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for running trails in Onda?
A: The running trails around Onda are quite varied. You'll find a mix of challenges, with 32 moderate routes and 39 difficult routes. There is also 1 easy route available, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the running trails in Onda?
A: Yes, many routes in Onda feature historical landmarks and offer scenic views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onda Castle – Sonella Bridge loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; provides views of Onda Castle. Other notable attractions in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Aragonesas&lt;/a&gt;, and the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2757154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aín Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Onda?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Onda are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880578306/running-circular-from-onda-to-les-penyes-aragoneses" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular from Onda to Les Penyes Aragoneses&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onda Castle – Sonella Bridge loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39539551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bàrbera – el Montí loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Onda?
A: The running community highly rates the trails in Onda, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the varied hill climbs, historical landmarks, and natural landscapes that define the experience.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs in Onda?
A: Yes, the region around Onda features several reservoirs that can be enjoyed. While specific running routes directly alongside them aren't detailed in every guide, you can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María Cristina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ajuez Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, offering potential scenic detours or starting points for your run.

Q: Are there family-friendly running trails in Onda?
A: While many routes in Onda feature challenging climbs, there is 1 easy route available, which might be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous run. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging routes in Onda?
A: Many outdoor trails in natural areas are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to check local regulations or specific route guidelines for any restrictions, especially regarding leashes or protected areas. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Onda?
A: One of the longer and more challenging options is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880578306/running-circular-from-onda-to-les-penyes-aragoneses" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular from Onda to Les Penyes Aragoneses&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 11.5 miles (18.6 km) and involves significant elevation gain through rocky hills.

Q: Are there any running routes with substantial climbs for a challenging workout?
A: Definitely. Onda is known for its hilly landscapes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39539551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bàrbera – el Montí loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39537857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Montí – Cova de Santa Bàrbera loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; are classified as difficult and include substantial climbs, perfect for runners seeking a rigorous challenge.

Q: How long does a typical running route in Onda take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly based on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onda Castle – Sonella Bridge loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; (6.6 miles) typically takes around 1 hour 8 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880578306/running-circular-from-onda-to-les-penyes-aragoneses" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular from Onda to Les Penyes Aragoneses&lt;/a&gt; (11.5 miles) can take about 2 hours 20 minutes.
tips: Valid for both TMB and gravel
